title: plantago paralias subsp. grisebachii
intro: Plantago paralias subsp. grisebachii, commonly known as sea plantain, is a subspecies of the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to coastal regions and sandy soils. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of sea plantain include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Sea plantain can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
